title: Authenticated Scope, Secrets, SSRF, Untrusted Content, and Privacy · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-authority secure crawl boundary and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Require explicit owner, account, role, purpose, paths, time, data, environment, and revocation authorization for sessions.
Acquire, scope, redact, rotate, destroy, and isolate secrets, cookies, CSRF material, caches, checkpoints, and reports per run and account.
Allow safe retrieval only, block state-changing forms, logout and destructive routes, and test unexpected GET side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Sensitive Retention
steps.instruction:
Order the Sensitive Retention Authorized site auditing and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Sensitive Retention.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Sensitive Retention assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Sensitive Retention, keep Sensitive Retention connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 2897 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives.

content.text: Order work so Sensitive Retention has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Sensitive Retention · fault isolation · Debug Sensitive Retention, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Sensitive Retention precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Sensitive Retention · fault isolation · Debug Sensitive Retention, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 9673 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Sensitive Retention · fault isolation · Debug Sensitive Retention, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Redacting final reports eliminates privacy risk from crawl state.

options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Sensitive Retention.

Another successful Sensitive Retention run of the same least-authority secure crawl boundary happy path. That claim fails the sensitive retention · fault isolation evidence boundary for Sensitive Retention.

For sensitive retention · fault isolation, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Sensitive Retention for least-authority secure crawl boundary.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Sensitive Retention
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-sensitive-retention-rp76br" that uses case 1358 to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Sensitive Retention.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sensitive Retention into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Sensitive Retention, keep Sensitive Retention connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Sensitive Retention structural check is only one gate. least-authority secure crawl boundary must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives.
